message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): HTTPS URL against plaintext server returns Err (tick 342)

Added `distributed_http_client_https_url_against_plaintext_server_returns_err`
— pins that `DistributedHttpClient::get` against an `https://` URL
whose server speaks plaintext HTTP returns `Err` rather than silently
downgrading or hanging.

Why this matters: URL scheme is a security-critical signal. A client
that silently stripped `https://` and connected plaintext (or silently
accepted a plaintext response on an https request) would defeat TLS
entirely — the caller asked for encrypted transport, got unencrypted,
and has no way to know anything went wrong.

The contract has three observable properties:
1. Result is `Err(_)` — caller sees the failure
2. Failure happens fast (<10s bound) — does not hang on handshake
3. No panic on TLS handshake failure with wrong server

Test uses a plaintext server that accepts and immediately closes so
the client's TLS handshake attempt gets either invalid handshake bytes
or EOF before completion, surfacing as Err.

This is the first scheme-dispatch contract test for the client. Prior
tests all used `http://` (plaintext loopback). Now a regression that
accidentally routed `https://` through the plaintext codepath would
fire loudly.

Test result: 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 19s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
